basics.name: Xiyun Hu
basics.label: Ph.D. Candidate in Mechanical Engineering
basics.summary: Ph.D. candidate in Mechanical Engineering with over four years of hands-on experience in machine learning and computer vision for augmented and mixed reality applications. My work develops AR/XR authoring tools, multimodal interaction systems, and learning/training interfaces that combine Python, PyTorch, OpenCV, Unity, and LLM-based workflows.

work.name: Fujitsu Research of America
highlights: Surveyed state-of-the-art human motion synthesis and video generation literature to identify research gaps and define a new experimental direction.
Developed a 3D human motion generation pipeline that integrates image generation, video generation, and human mesh recovery to reconstruct temporally consistent 3D motion from text and 2D inputs.
Designed and prototyped a user interface for interactive control of the motion generation pipeline and evaluated usability and motion quality.

publications.title: ARify: Leveraging Narrated Instructional Videos to Create Augmented Reality Tutorials for Procedural Tasks
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems

publications.title: agentAR: Creating Augmented Reality Applications with Tool-Augmented LLM-based Autonomous Agents
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 38th Annual ACM Symposium on User Interface Software and Technology

publications.title: GesPrompt: Leveraging Co-Speech Gestures to Augment LLM-Based Interaction in Virtual Reality
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 2025 ACM Designing Interactive Systems Conference

publications.title: Ubi Edge: Authoring Edge-Based Opportunistic Tangible User Interfaces in Augmented Reality
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the 2023 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems

projects.name: ARify
projects.summary: Semi-automatic AR authoring system that converts narrated instructional videos into spatially anchored AR tutorials.
highlights: Integrated object detection, segmentation, pose estimation, hand tracking, and VLM-based task parsing.
Built a desktop authoring workflow for refining tutorial structures and spatial placement of AR guidance elements.

projects.name: GesPrompt
projects.summary: Multimodal XR interaction workflow that combines speech, gesture recognition, and LLM-based prompt tuning.
highlights: Designed a co-speech gesture framework that extracts spatial parameters from gestures to clarify ambiguous speech commands.
Improved XR interaction accuracy by jointly processing gesture and speech inputs through an LLM agent.

projects.name: agentAR
projects.summary: Tool-augmented LLM agent that supports natural-language AR application creation.
highlights: Built a machine-learning tool library for agent-based AR workflows.
Tested robustness by recreating existing AR applications and creating new applications with different ML algorithm combinations.
